---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Molecular Biology · Neuroscience · Bioinformatics"
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-title: "On This Page"
---

Undergraduate research experience spanning wet-lab neuroscience, molecular biology, and computational genomics. This background informs my current focus on building reproducible multi-omics pipelines for pharmacogenomic analysis.

---

## Undergraduate Research {#undergrad}

### Digitization and Quantification of CGRP-IR Axons in Cardiac Nervous System

Dr. Zixi Cheng | Department of Biomedical Sciences | UCF  
June 2022 – May 2023

::: {.summary}
Performed 3D neuron reconstruction and quantification of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) immunoreactive axons in mouse cardiac tissue using confocal microscopy and computational image analysis. Created the first 2D/3D digitized tracings of CGRP-IR axons across whole mouse heart chambers, expanding limited existing datasets on cardiac nociceptive innervation patterns.
:::

**Key contributions:**
- Immunohistochemical staining and tissue preparation for flat-mount imaging
- 3D axonal tracing using Neurolucida software across four cardiac chambers
- Quantitative analysis pipeline development in ImageJ/Fiji
- Data visualization and statistical analysis in GraphPad Prism

[View Poster](files/SSS-Poster_CG_Cheng.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}

**Methods:** Confocal microscopy · Neurolucida · ImageJ · GraphPad Prism · Adobe Illustrator

---

### Mutant U1A-RNA Binding Kinetics with Surface Plasmon Resonance

Dr. Robert Borgon | Department of Biomedical Sciences | UCF                                         
August 2022 – May 2023

::: {.summary}
Characterized binding kinetics of U1A protein mutants to U1 hairpin II RNA using surface plasmon resonance (SPR). Identified functionally significant amino acid residues in the RNA recognition motif by comparing kinetic constants (k<sub>a</sub>, k<sub>d</sub>, K<sub>D</sub>) across wildtype and point mutant variants.
:::

**Key contributions:**
- Recombinant protein expression and purification with His-tag affinity chromatography
- SPR kinetic measurements on NTA sensor chips
- Kinetic modeling and curve fitting in TraceDrawer
- Comparative proteomic analysis of RRM domains across U1 snRNP subunits

[View Poster](files/SURE_Poster_Borgon.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}

**Methods:** Surface plasmon resonance · PCR · Bacterial culture · R · TraceDrawer · GraphPad Prism

---

### Microbiome Analysis of Ophiocordyceps-Infected Ants

Dr. Anna Forsman | Genomics & Bioinformatics | UCF  
January 2022 – May 2022

::: {.summary}
Analyzed gut microbiome composition differences between carpenter ants infected with behavior-manipulating fungi (Ophiocordyceps), non-manipulating pathogenic fungi (Beauveria), and controls. Performed gDNA extraction, two-step PCR amplification, Illumina sequencing, and taxonomic profiling via QIIME 2 to assess microbial community shifts associated with fungal infection.
:::

**Methods:** Illumina sequencing · PCR · QIIME 2 · Gel electrophoresis

---

## Research Direction {#interests}

Current work focuses on **neuropharmacogenomics** — specifically, how genetic variation in drug metabolism enzymes (CYP2D6, CYP2C19) correlates with brain transcriptomic and epigenomic profiles in psychiatric disorders. This builds on wet-lab molecular biology foundations while developing the computational skills (multi-omics integration, reproducible pipelines, machine learning) needed for independent research in this area.

The PsychPGx-MultiOmics project represents the transition from supporting wet-lab research to designing and executing full computational biology analyses end-to-end.

[View Current Project →](projects/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
